title: noccaea dolichocarpa
intro: Noccaea dolichocarpa, also known as alpine penny-cress, is a plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Noccaea dolichocarpa has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions, like eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Noccaea dolichocarpa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their health benefits.
